Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Markus Spiske on UnsplashEvery Flutter application stores data.
Some store:

• User preferences

• Theme settings

• Language selection
Others store far more sensitive information:

• Authentication tokens

• User profiles

• Session identifiers

• Banking information

• Offline business data
While storing data locally improves performance and enables offline experiences, it also introduces security risks.
A common mistake is assuming that every piece of data belongs in SharedPreferences.
It doesn’t.
Choosing the wrong storage mechanism can expose sensitive information if a device is compromised or an attacker inspects the application’s local files.
In this article, we’ll explore Flutter’s local storage options, understand when each should be used, and learn production-ready strategies for protecting sensitive data on users’ devices.
Why Local Storage Matters
